title: Milla bivalvis
intro: Milla bivalvis, also known as the two-leafed Milla or false lilyturf, is a plant species in the family Asparagaceae. The health benefits of Milla bivalvis include its potential in treating various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Milla bivalvis is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory problems and other conditions.
taxonomy: Milla bivalvis is classified within the family Agavaceae and the order Asparagales. This classification places Milla bivalvis in the monocot clade, which includes plants with a single cotyledon in their seeds. Further, Milla bivalvis is a genus of flowering plants that is native to Central and South America. The classification of Milla bivalvis is often used in botanical and taxonomic studies to understand the relationships between plant species. The Agavaceae family is a group of plants that are commonly known as agaves or agave relatives.
